name: Cheese Monkey Bread- La Times
recipeIngredient: 1 cup butter, plus extra for greasing bowl and mold
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix yeast with warm water and sugar until dissolved.
2. Scald milk in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
3. Remove from heat and stir in butter and salt. Allow mixture to cool to lukewarm.
4. Add beaten eggs and yeast mixture to the cooled milk mixture. Mix well.
5. Gradually add flour and cheese with a wooden spoon until a soft dough forms.
6. If the dough is too soft, gradually add more flour until the desired consistency is reached.
7. Transfer dough to a greased bowl and turn to grease all over. Cover and let rise in a warm place free from drafts until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
8. Divide dough into two portions. On a floured surface, roll each portion into a rectangle that is 1/3-inch thick.
9. Melt the remaining 1/2 cup butter. Dip each piece of dough into melted butter and layer them in a greased 10-inch ring mold, overlapping points. There should be about three layers, but the pan should not be more than three-quarters full or the dough will pop off while baking.
10. Let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
11. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Bake for about 35 minutes, or until lightly browned.
6 servings. Each serving: 643 calories; 847 mg sodium; 207 mg cholesterol; 39 grams fat; 56 grams carbohydrates; 16 grams protein; 0.20 gram fiber.
